entry.title: Washington Post: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine live updates: Turkey drops opposition to Finland, Sweden joining NATO
  • Turkey drops opposition to Finland and Sweden joining NATO
  • Biden, Macron, Scholz, Johnson: Allies abroad, struggling at home

  • After Ukraine mall strike, witnesses describe scene of ‘hell’
  • Scotland’s leader seeks new independence vote in October 2023
  • Ex-Nazi guard convicted of over 3,500 counts of accessory to murder
  • Mapping the worldwide spread of the coronavirus
  • Colombia prison fire kills at least 52, authorities say
  • Brazen heist caught on video amid major global arts fair
  • Israel loosens abortion law restrictions after Roe v. Wade decision
  • China slashes covid quarantine for travelers, but remains outlier
  • Venezuela tapped 1.5 million phone lines. It’s just the start, experts warn.
  • Indian Muslim journalist, critic of Modi, arrested over tweets
  • Resistance builds in Tunisia as populist leader seeks more power
  • Chlorine gas leak at port in Jordan kills at least 13, injures hundreds
  • Rural rhythms upended by war on the road to Ukraine’s front lines
  • Burials, exhumations, outrage: A day in the endless Philippine drug war
  • At crucial summits, the West squeezes Russia
  • Leonardo Del Vecchio, Italian titan of eyewear, dies at 87

  • Higher fuel prices spark social unrest in South America

  • Soaring inflation and $6.30 gas spur relief checks in California
  • GOP Rep. Boebert: ‘I’m tired of this separation of church and state junk’

  • ‘ER’ actress Mary Mara dies after apparent drowning in New York river

  • LAPD officer suffers injuries in training, leading to death, family says
  • 46 migrants found dead in Texas inside sweltering tractor-trailer
  • In Hutchinson’s testimony, experts see ‘nuggets’ for Justice probe

  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Who is Cassidy Hutchinson?
  • Frustration, anger rising among Democrats over caution on abortion
  • Post Politics Now: Jan. 6 committee to hold hearing Tuesday on ‘recently obtained evidence’
  • Agents seize phone of lawyer who pushed Trump false elector claims
  • Drugstores ration Plan B contraceptives as Roe ruling sparks panic
  • Ernst & Young hit with $100 million fine over cheating on ethics tests
  • Taiwan official calls for approval of U.S. computer chip subsidies
  • Sony targets PC gamers with new hardware brand, Inzone
  • Pro-Trump web raced to debunk Jan. 6 testimony. Then they got confused.
  • The AI tool behind Thanos made facial animation in ‘The Quarry’ a snap
  • Roomy, adaptable townhouses rising in Clarksburg, Md.
  • Which roof will serve you best through the years?
  • 12 tips for home maintenance during the summer
  • Trump’s Final Scene Didn’t Go According to Script
  • Human Rights on Mars Won’t Be the Same as Those on Earth
  • Credit Suisse Reveals Where It Went Wrong
  • Average Investors Should Try and Time Markets
  • How Russia’s Invasion of Ukraine Pushed Finland and Sweden Toward NATO
  • What Is the ‘Special Debt’ China Uses to Spur Its Economy?
  • The Legal Obstacle to Federal Abortion Efforts After Supreme Court’s Roe v. Wade Ruling
  • How Putin’s Hopes to Weaken NATO Did the Opposite
  • An aide shatters the Trump White House’s code of silence
  • Republicans could be taking a victory lap. Here’s why they aren’t.
  • How the West can (still) be one
  • Cassidy Hutchinson just gave us a smoking gun. But will Republicans care?
  • If Kavanaugh and Gorsuch lied in their confirmations, so did Kagan
  • Susan Collins needs to own up to her decision to back Kavanaugh
  • Can’t stand all the political heat? Escape to the park.
  • No, Donald Trump probably can’t take down Kevin McCarthy
  • Raphael Warnock’s push for a new America
  • Jan. 6 testimony shows that Donald Trump is unhinged. Voters must listen.
  • The Supreme Court is eroding the wall between church and state
  • More damning details emerge about the police response in Uvalde
  • 5 stunning revelations about Jan. 6 from Cassidy Hutchinson
  • Bombshell hearing raises a question: Why was Trump’s lawyer terrified?
  • The Supreme Court’s gun ruling was a victory over racist policing
  • Trump’s on fire
  • Taking the wheel
  • Hong Kong was not supposed to look like this
  • Canada decriminalizing drugs in British Columbia will prolong suffering
  • Maryland’s offshore wind will help the climate and create jobs
  • Let the Latin Mass remain in D.C.

  • Who will protect these mothers?

  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Financial Times: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 28 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-28 21:58PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • More than 10 journalists denied permission to cover Hong Kong handover anniversary events due to security reasons, reporters’ group says
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong may cut hotel quarantine to 5 days, with 2 more in home isolation, incoming health minister says
  • Maritime authorities yet to announce Jumbo Floating Restaurant’s fate as Hong Kong expert says sinking it might not be possible
  • 3,000 guests, staff to begin hotel quarantine in Hong Kong ahead of Chinese President Xi Jinping’s visit and July 1 celebrations
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum ‘ready to be fully opened’ on July 2, with security checks in place
  • Taiwanese drinks shop owners jailed for up to 7 months over social media posts calling on others to flout Hong Kong’s Covid-19 curbs
  • Carrie Lam makes final comments on social media as Hong Kong’s leader, will shut down accounts after stepping down on Thursday
  • BioNTech may provide Covid-19 vaccine targeting Omicron to Hong Kong as early as third quarter of year if approved; city logs 1,685 cases
  • Hong Kong to ban drones as part of security blanket for Xi Jinping’s visit to mark 25th anniversary of handover
  • First-ever Hong Kong Science Fair Supports Hong Kong’s Innovation Development
  • Hong Kong opposition camp rues its future: will it be able to find its place in new political landscape?
  • Priced out of Hong Kong, young home buyers look to Greater Bay Area for space and value
  • Can Hong Kong’s next leader John Lee fix the city’s housing crisis?
  • US ‘smart city’ tech highlights contrasts with China over privacy and control
  • China-Australia relations: chance for ‘thaw’, but can Beijing, Canberra mend fractured trade ties?
  • US seeks China pressure on Russia to end Ukraine war as it weighs economic options against Beijing
  • Global stagflation risks offer China chance to ‘seize opportunity’ for economic, trade windfall
  • G7 leaders denounce China over Russia relations, human rights and Taiwan friction in statement following summit
  • Quarter of world’s population at risk from once-in-a-century floods
  • Chinese breakthrough lets human brains beam radio waves
  • Chinese video gaming billionaire plans relocation with family abroad after draconian Shanghai lockdown
  • Apple supplier Foxconn starts new hiring spree at world’s largest iPhone factory in China ahead of next product launch
  • China, Nato and how the Ukraine war is spilling over into the Asia-Pacific
  • Australians view China as more a security threat than economic partner, Lowy Institute poll shows
  • Beijing would be wise to learn from the outcry over misuse of Covid-19 health codes
  • China’s vice-president to attend Marcos inauguration in Philippines
  • Taiwan slams Beijing for blocking UN Ocean Conference delegates
  • Africa would welcome G7’s US$600 billion infrastructure push – if it happens
  • China pledges quake aid for Afghanistan but also wants Taliban to take action on terrorism
  • Which tech firms qualify as ‘little giants’? Beijing releases criteria for the ideal industrial enterprise
  • Coronavirus: China eases quarantine for overseas arrivals based on ‘lessons learned’ in Shanghai
  • Evergrande crisis: embattled developer could face liquidation as creditor files winding-up petition in Hong Kong
  • China’s H-6 bombers in show of air-navy coordination near southern Japan, military experts say
  • Xi Jinping points to public opinion campaign as example of ‘whole-process democracy’
  • China’s yuan liquidity reserve pool highlights efforts to loosen US dollar hegemony
  • Australia says committed to Southeast Asia’s stability, allays fears over Aukus nuclear submarines
  • Philippines’ Marcos Jnr will take presidential oath at venue linked to father’s brutal legacy
  • Divers angered by Vietnam ban to protect damaged coral reefs, blaming fishing boats for the problem
  • Australian PM warns China to learn from Russia’s ‘strategic failure’ in Ukraine
  • Thailand tops 2 million tourists this year, as it longs for absent Chinese visitors
  • Australia-China relations: Canberra to set up Pacific defence school as Beijing seeks rival regional meeting
  • Singapore man who preyed on disabled children jailed for record 45 years in ‘exceptionally sickening’ case
  • Malaysian immigration slammed over deaths of 149 Indonesians: ‘No clean water, atrocious food’
  • Türkiye lifts its objections to Sweden, Finland joining Nato – triggered by Russia’s invasion of Ukraine
  • Ronald Reagan shooter says sorry for wounding former president and injuring 3 others
  • Donald Trump tried to strangle Secret Service agent in attempt to reach Capitol on day of riots, aide testifies
  • Amid heatwaves and floods, time is running out for rich nations to fulfil climate pledges and avert catastrophe
  • Ghislaine Maxwell sentenced to 20 years for helping Jeffrey Epstein sexually abuse teenage girls
  • 2 arrested after armed jewel heist at art fair in Netherlands
  • Supermarket boss says Britons buying more cheap frozen food as cost of living crisis worsens
  • ‘Died as they danced’: Speculation swirls in South Africa over tragic teen party
  • Accused ‘duck sauce killer’ of Chinese food delivery worker in New York City released on US$500,000 bail
  • US commerce secretary urges lawmakers to pass US$52 billion chip-making bill, warning companies could turn elsewhere
  • US probes ‘horrifying and heartbreaking’ deaths of 50 migrants found in sweltering lorry
  • Chlorine gas blast at Jordan port kills at least 12, injures over 250
  • How an expanded BRICS could lead the world instead of the waning West
  • FBI agents seize phone of Donald Trump’s election lawyer
  • Misguided geopolitics is derailing decades of globalisation, pushing the most vulnerable to brink of disaster
  • China and Hong Kong mark 25th anniversary of handover with launch of ETF Connect on July 4, ‘a gift’ that will bring their capital markets even closer
  • Hong Kong’s luxury home rents fall as expats leave in droves due to Covid curbs, while New York, Toronto and Singapore see gains
  • Shell, ExxonMobil join study to build one of the world’s largest carbon capture and storage projects in China
  • Shanghai reopening: Disneyland theme park to reopen on June 30, ending its 101-day hiatus as life in China’s commercial hub ambles back to normal
  • Hong Kong index of lived-in home prices fell in May and could decline further in June amid high supply of new flats, interest rate rise concerns
  • Chinese spicy snack giant Weilong Delicious seeks to relaunch Hong Kong IPO targeting US$500 million, says source
  • What does gloomy US and China economic outlook mean for stock prices?
  • Why Hong Kong landlords, developers and asset managers cannot ignore ESG standards
  • Haidilao, Macau casino stocks lead Hong Kong market rally on China border reopening hopes
  • Taiwan’s GlobalWafers plans US$5 billion silicon wafer plant in Texas after failed European investment
  • Rooftop farms in Hong Kong skyscrapers provide fruitful and nutritious hobby to stressed-out urbanites
  • Use of bitcoin, other digital money to buy property grinds to halt as cryptocurrency market crashes
  • Chinese stocks’ US$1.8 trillion recovery from April low faces another earnings test as UBS, Citic predict setbacks
  • Racism and hate have no place in K-pop fandoms, but are rife. What can we do?
  • Prior Convictions movie review: Japanese manga adaptation sees Kasumi Arimura reprise her role from TV series as a volunteer probation officer
  • Liquid rhinoplasty: pros and cons of the scalpel-free nose job that is trending in 2022
  • Hong Kong dance performance draws on sense of helplessness over politics and the pandemic, and families’ tough decision to stay or go
  • Korean soup, stews and broths: all you need to know about the dishes that represent the very lifeblood of a nation
  • Alcohol-free drinks events a growing trend, drawing the sober curious and health-focused people
  • Hong Kong singer Mike Tsang on performing with George Lam, his new album, and who he would love to collaborate with
  • 10 of the longest-lasting LGBT celebrity couples: from Michael Kors’ intern love affair and Elton John’s four-decade relationship, to RuPaul’s long-distance marriage and George Takei’s 18-year secret
  • Inside 4 tech billionaires’ new Malibu mansions: Google’s Sergey Brin, WhatsApp’s Jan Koum, Apple’s Laurene Powell Jobs and venture capitalist Marc Andreessen splashed millions on beachfront homes
  • Inside Kris Jenner’s extravagant car collection, including Mercedes-Benzes, Bentleys and Rolls-Royces – but why did Ferrari ban the Kardashians from buying its limited-edition cars?
  • Who is British billionaire Alan Howard? Lady Gaga performed at his wedding, a US$12 million ceremony on Lake Como – he also splurges on NFTs, crypto and Monet artworks
  • Paris Fashion Week: Rick Owens’ dramatic spring/summer 2023 menswear show had everything from fire and neon to bare chests and a leather made from Brazilian fish scales
  • Hermès brought summer holiday vibes to Paris Fashion Week with pastel colours and a contemporary take on Birkin’s ancestor – the Haut à courroies travel bag – for its menswear spring/summer 2023 show
  • Meet Chinese billionaire Weili Dai, one of the US’s richest self-made women: the Shanghai-born ‘technology geek’ made her money through semiconductors, joining Kim K and Rihanna on Forbes’ 2022 list
  • STYLE Edit: Omega releases the newest Mini Trésor collection in celebration of its star-studded campaign, featuring inspiring women including Nicole Kidman, Zoë Kravitz, Kaia Gerber and Zhou Dongyu
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.
